31 yr old Nepali - (British national) -  In the UK and other global economies, the rapid advancement of AI and digital systems is transforming the job landscape, leading to significant shifts in employment. Roles traditionally reliant on human input, such as administrative coding, web development, IT administration, social media management, video editing, graphic design, accountancy, and even positions like purchasing managers and administrative staff, are increasingly being automated and AI. This trend has led to notable job reductions across various sectors; for instance, PwC has released 5,000 employees, Citigroup has reduced its workforce by 4,000 to 5,000, and similar actions have been taken by Barclays, HSBC, and numerous other firms within the accounting, technology, and communications industries. In response to these changes, professionals are encouraged to pivot towards careers that are more resistant to AI disruption, focusing on areas where human expertise and creativity remain irreplaceable.
